channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: GOP senators push ahead on Trump's tax cuts package, punting big decisions for later
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Republicans said they are pushing ahead on President Donald Trump'sbig bill of tax breaks and spending cuts this week, even though they're punting some of the most difficult decisions — including the costs and how to pay for the multitrillion-dollar package — until later.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic-backed Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat, cementing liberal majority
item.description: Susan Crawford, a Dane County judge who led legal fights to protect union power and abortion rights and to oppose voter ID, defeated Republican-backed Brad Schimel in a race that broke records for spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Milwaukee polling places running out of ballots amid 'historic turnout' by voters
item.description: Milwaukee is facing a ballot shortage due to "historic turnout" as Wisconsin voters decide the ideological direction of the state's Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican state Sen. Randy Fine wins special election for Florida's 6th Congressional District
item.description: Republican state Sen. Randy Fine won a special election Tuesday to represent Florida's 6th Congressional District, defeating Democratic challenger Josh Weil and squashing efforts by national Democrats who spent millions of dollars on the race.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans seek to block the reappointment of UN official accused of antisemitism
item.description: The House Foreign Affairs Committee is pushing the United Nations to not reappoint Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese for another term over accusations of antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration halts dozens of research grants at Princeton University
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has halted dozens of research grants at Princeton University, the latest Ivy League school to see its federal money threatened in a pressure campaign targeting the nation’s top universities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mass layoffs are underway at the nation's public health agencies
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department received notices Tuesday that their jobs were being eliminated, part of a sweeping overhaul designed to vastly shrink the agencies responsible for protecting and promoting Americans’ health.
item.dc:creator: CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zeldin to visit border where 'disgusting Mexican sewage' is harming US environment
item.description: Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Administrator Lee Zeldin said he plans to visit the border to assess toxic waste spilling over from Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Speaker Johnson suffers a defeat in his push to block parents in the U.S. House from proxy voting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Speaker Mike Johnson endured a decisive defeat Tuesday after he staged an unusually aggressive effort to squash a proposal for new parents in Congress to able to vote by proxy, rather than in person, as they care for newborns.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and LEAH ASKARINAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top scientists warn that Trump policies are causing a ‘climate of fear’ in research
item.description: Some 2,000 scientists, including dozens of Nobel Prize winners, have signed an open letter warning that the U.S. lead in science is being “decimated” by the Trump administration’s cuts to research.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Republicans, Democrats trade barbs in heated hearing on activist judges blocking Trump agenda
item.description: The House Judiciary Committee held a hearing on judges blocking the Trump administration's agenda on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Tonight's elections are a Trump, Musk referendum
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Emerging GOP leader backing Trump’s use of Alien Enemies Act at Supreme Court
item.description: Congressman Brandon Gill is teaming with America First Legal to file an amicus brief to the Supreme Court backing President Donald Trump’s use of the Alien Enemies Act to deport violent gang members.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Melania Trump says courage is 'based in love' as she honors women from around the world for bravery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Courage is based in love, Melania Trump said Tuesday as she recognized eight women from around the world for bravery, including an Israeli citizen who was held captive by Hamas for nearly two months after the militant group's deadly raid on her country in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans torpedo Johnson effort to block proxy voting for new parents
item.description: The House GOP battle over proxy voting for new parents escalated to a full-blown procedural war Tuesday that will have ramifications for Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) ability to control his razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge in long-running lawsuit declines to block the use of Georgia's voting system
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A federal judge has declined to block the use of Georgia's electronic voting system in a long-running lawsuit alleging that the system is vulnerable to attack and has operational issues that could deprive voters of their constitutional rights. U.S.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major union launches ad campaign featuring detained Tufts student
item.description: A major labor union launched a six-figure ad campaign on Tuesday featuring Tufts University Ph.D. student Rumeysa Ozturk decrying what it called an infringement on the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican Jimmy Patronis wins Matt Gaetz's Florida District 1 seat: DDHQ
item.description: Valimont raised almost $6.5 million compared to Patronis' $2.1 million, according to Federal Elections Commission filings.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrat will block Trump’s VA nominees to protest cuts
item.description: Sen. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) says he will put a hold on President Trump’s nominees to lead the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to protest cuts at the agency that provides lifelong health care services to military veterans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Legislation aimed at protecting Florida state parks stuck in committee
item.description: What all started with a leaked proposal to build golf courses and pickleball courts at state parks last year has now resulted in legislation that aims to protect natural grounds from those very acts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US auto companies can eat costs of tariffs on parts: Trump
item.description: President Trump's senior trade adviser, Peter Navarro, says the tariffs plan will raise more than $6 trillion in federal revenue over the next decade.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: 6 things borrowers should know about federal student loans right now
item.description: NPR has spent the past few weeks catching up with student loan experts and asking the Trump administration for clarity on some of borrowers’ biggest questions.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Trump won't rule out military force to take Greenland
item.description: In an interview with NBC News, Trump refused to rule out military force to acquire Greenland. His remarks come a day after Vice President JD Vance's scaled back visit to the island.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin's attorney general asks the state Supreme Court to stop Musk's $1 million payments
item.description: Wisconsin’s attorney general has asked the liberal-controlled state Supreme Court to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters. The request comes hours before President Donald Trump’s ally plans the giveaway at an evening rally in Green Bay.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Illinois to defy Trump voter order for April election
item.description: President Trump earlier this week issued an executive order calling for all voters to provide proof of citizenship before receiving a ballot.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Congress questions the FAA, US Army and NTSB over deadly midair collision in DC
item.description: The head of the National Transportation Safety Board appeared alongside officials from the Federal Aviation Administration and the Army's aviation division at a hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5
